title: Fix timezone handling in scheduled job timestamp
description: The scheduler was comparing a naive datetime with a timezone-aware datetime, causing a TypeError at midnight UTC. Made both datetimes timezone-aware.
diff:
diff --git a/scheduler/cron.py b/scheduler/cron.py
index 3e4f5a6..b7c8d9e 100644
--- a/scheduler/cron.py
+++ b/scheduler/cron.py
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
-from datetime import datetime
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
 
 
 def is_due(last_run: datetime, interval_seconds: int) -> bool:
@@ -8,5 +8,5 @@ def is_due(last_run: datetime, interval_seconds: int) -> bool:
     if last_run is None:
         return True
 
-    elapsed = (datetime.utcnow() - last_run).total_seconds()
+    elapsed = (datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc) - last_run.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)).total_seconds()
     return elapsed >= interval_seconds
comments: yuri: This was causing the midnight crash in the cron runner. Approve.
author: adam
